serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of chicken and mushroom noodle soup is approximately 400 g, comprising broth, egg noodles, sliced chicken, and black mushrooms as typically served at a Singapore hawker centre or food court.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken and Mushroom Noodles (Soup) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chicken and Mushroom Noodles (Soup), CR ID F05060024. Iron value is reported as -1 (not available) in the source record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — egg noodles, boiled (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken and mushroom noodle soup. GI of 47 is estimated from boiled egg noodles (GI ~47, low category) per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (47 × 6.7 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 400 g serving) / 100 = 12.6 g. The soup dilution effect further reduces the effective glycemic impact per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from broth and seasoning; a 400 g bowl delivers approximately 1092 mg sodium, representing 47–55% of the recommended daily limit for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced broth volume, lighter seasoning, and low-sodium soy sauce; consuming only half the broth can reduce sodium intake by approximately 200–300 mg per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from refined egg noodles (6.7 g/100 g); GI is low (~47) due to soup dilution and protein co-ingestion, but a full bowl still delivers approximately 26.8 g carbohydrate.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should consume a standard bowl in moderation, prioritise the broth, chicken, and mushrooms over noodles, and request wholegrain noodles where available to reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 273 mg/100 g is the primary cardiovascular concern; fat content is low (1.0 g/100 g) and saturated fat is minimal (0.3 g/100 g), which is favourable, but cumulative sodium from a full bowl is clinically significant.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit broth consumption to reduce sodium load, and may benefit from the mushroom beta-glucan content which has modest LDL-lowering properties; overall fat profile is heart-friendly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Black mushrooms provide beta-glucans with immune-modulating anti-inflammatory properties; saturated fat is very low (0.3 g/100 g) and the soup preparation avoids AGE-generating high-heat cooking.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; request reduced seasoning and soy sauce to minimise sodium-related tissue swelling, and ensure generous mushroom portions for maximum beta-glucan benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken and Mushroom Noodle Soup is a broth-based noodle dish consisting of boiled egg noodles served in a savoury chicken broth with sliced or shredded chicken and black mushrooms (shiitake or wood ear variety). It is a common hawker centre and food court offering in Singapore, typically served in a bowl of approximately 400 g. The dish has a mild, umami-rich flavour from the mushrooms and broth, with a soft noodle texture and tender chicken pieces. Nutritionally, it is relatively low in energy (53 kcal/100 g) and fat (1.0 g/100 g), with a moderate protein content (4.4 g/100 g) and low carbohydrate density (6.7 g/100 g) due to the diluting effect of the broth. Sodium is the primary clinical concern at 273 mg/100 g, translating to approximately 1092 mg per standard 400 g bowl — a significant proportion of the daily sodium limit for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 47 (low) based on boiled egg noodles, making it a relatively moderate glycemic option for individuals managing type 2 diabetes. Black mushrooms contribute dietary fibre (0.7 g/100 g) and beta-glucans with anti-inflammatory and modest cholesterol-lowering properties. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore hawker contexts and is dairy-free. It is not suitable for gluten-free diets due to egg noodles. Iron data is not available in the HPB source record.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F05060024), which is the primary approved source for Singapore hawker dishes. Source match is exact. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using boiled egg noodles as the closest approved reference, which is clearly documented.
notes: iron_mg is set to -1: the HPB Singapore Foodo source record for F05060024 reports iron as not available (-1); no supplementary approved source (USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN) was used to substitute this value to preserve source fidelity. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this composite soup dish is not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Saturated fat of 0.3 g/100 g is estimated from the HPB source context for this dish type; the HPB record does not separately itemise saturated fat but the total fat of 1.0 g/100 g with a chicken-and-mushroom composition supports this estimate. Cultural tags in the input record listed 'western' as primary cuisine; this has been corrected to 'Singaporean / Asian fusion' as the dish is a standard Singapore hawker offering. The input also listed 'vegetarian' as a dietary tag; this has been removed as the dish contains chicken. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (47 × 6.7 × 400) / 10000 = 12.6 g for a 400 g bowl.
